findings.title: Payable proxy deployment paths accept ETH with no initializer and can strand native funds in the proxy

best_agent_match.title: Payable proxy setup paths accept ETH even when no initializer runs, permanently trapping native funds

rejected_candidates.title: Delegatecall-based initialization and migrations can assign privileged roles to the deployer/factory or ProxyAdmin instead of the intended operator
rejected_candidates.reason: This behavior is a known property of proxy `delegatecall` initialization, but no implementation/factory code in this codebase derives privileged roles from `msg.sender`. Without a concrete proxied implementation that misuses `msg.sender`, this is an integration caution rather than a reportable bug in the supplied contracts.

rejected_candidates.title: Upgrade control contracts expose irreversible ownership renunciation that can permanently disable incident-response upgrades
rejected_candidates.reason: `renounceOwnership()` is standard, intentional `Ownable` behavior and only triggers through an authorized owner action. In this codebase it does not create an unintended permission bypass or protocol-specific exploit path, so it is not reportable as a vulnerability.
